evidence.scientific_clarity: All three required equations (ELBO, KL decomposition, retrieval-augmented objective) are present with connecting prose that explains each derivation step; matches brief exactly.
evidence.typography: Latin Modern Type 1 fonts (LMRoman, LMMathItalic, LMMathSymbols) throughout; no DejaVu Sans; all fonts embedded and subsetted; proper upright operators (KL, log) vs italic variables.
evidence.palette: Monochrome math derivation — no color used; no colorblind or directional/categorical palette concerns, but color could have highlighted the new contribution (Eq. 3).
evidence.layout_density: Single letter-page filled with title, three prose paragraphs, and three display equations; no gaping whitespace; underbrace annotations label reconstruction and KL terms inline.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption labels (eq:elbo, eq:decomp, eq:retro) match \label{} tags in the document; title present; no legend needed; cross-references resolve correctly.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdfTeX 1.40.29 produces real vector PDF; all 15 font instances are Type 1 embedded+subsetted (not Type 3 bitmap); pdftotext extracts full text confirming selectable glyphs.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (180 KB), figure.png (1275x1650 px, 164 KB), caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial; equations.tex (required by brief) is absent from the output directory.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption correctly names each equation and its role in the derivation chain, but contains no specific numerical claim or quantitative finding (e.g., value of k, convergence bound, or empirical result).

overall_strengths: Mathematically rigorous LaTeX typesetting with Latin Modern fonts, proper operator styling, and fully embedded Type 1 fonts — publication-ready vector PDF.
Content precisely fulfils the brief: three numbered equations with explanatory prose, \eqref cross-references, and underbrace annotations that make the decomposition self-explanatory.
overall_weaknesses: equations.tex (the standalone .tex source) is missing from the output directory, violating the brief's explicit file-output requirements.
Caption is descriptive but purely qualitative — it lacks a concrete numerical claim or specific finding that would anchor the figure for a reader skimming the paper.
